metadata.purpose: Pre-coding mapping for Broker reference data - ADR-013

api_contract.description: Returns list of valid broker names for use in select dropdowns. API returns brokers filtered by user_id/tenant. If no user-specific data exists, defaults JSON file is loaded.

value.description: Broker name value (singular, used as form value)

label.description: Broker display label (for UI, can be same as value)

forms_using_broker_select.notes: Required field - must select from list

implementation_notes.select_population: Select options will be populated dynamically from GET /api/v1/reference/brokers endpoint
implementation_notes.value_mapping: Form will submit 'value' field from API response
implementation_notes.display_mapping: UI will display 'label' field from API response
implementation_notes.fallback_behavior: If API fails, form must display an error message to the user. No fallback to manual text input is allowed. The select field must remain disabled until the API call succeeds. This ensures compliance with ADR-013: broker list source must be API-based only. Note: Backend will use defaults JSON if user has no broker-specific data, but Frontend should not implement client-side fallback to manual entry.
implementation_notes.caching: Consider caching broker list in frontend (sessionStorage or memory) to reduce API calls and improve performance. Cache should be invalidated on page refresh or when explicitly needed.

valid_brokers_list.notes: This is an initial reference list for defaults JSON. The actual API endpoint returns user-specific brokers first (from user_data.brokers_fees WHERE user_id = current_user.id), and if empty, falls back to this defaults list. Each user sees only their own brokers plus defaults if they have none.

team_20_responsibilities: Define response schema matching the contract above
Filter brokers by user_id/tenant (per-user data source)
If no user-specific brokers exist, load defaults from JSON file (defaults_brokers.json)
Ensure authentication is required (JWT Bearer token) - user_id extracted from JWT
Return brokers in consistent format (value/label structure)
No fallback to manual text input - API-based source only (ADR-013 compliance)

data_source_logic.query: SELECT DISTINCT broker FROM user_data.brokers_fees WHERE user_id = :user_id AND deleted_at IS NULL
data_source_logic.fallback_trigger: If primary query returns empty result (no user-specific brokers)
data_source_logic.fallback_content: Contains the valid_brokers_list from this mapping file
data_source_logic.implementation: 1. Query user-specific brokers from DB. 2. If empty, load defaults_brokers.json. 3. Return combined or defaults-only list.
data_source_logic.no_manual_fallback: No fallback to manual text input allowed - API-based source only (ADR-013 compliance)

defaults_file.structure: Same as valid_brokers_list in this mapping file
defaults_file.purpose: Fallback when user has no broker-specific data
defaults_file.maintenance: Updated manually or via migration scripts when broker list changes

team_30_responsibilities: Replace text input with select dropdown in D16 Trading Accounts form
Replace text input with select dropdown in D18 Brokers Fees form
Fetch broker list from GET /api/v1/reference/brokers on form load
Map API response (value/label) to select options
Implement error handling for API failures

select_implementation.empty_option: Include empty option for D16 (optional field): <option value=''>-- בחר ברוקר --</option>
select_implementation.loading_state: Show loading indicator while fetching broker list
select_implementation.error_state: Show error message if API call fails. Do not allow fallback to text input. Select field must remain disabled until API call succeeds. Display user-friendly error message explaining that broker selection is temporarily unavailable.

compliance.phoenix_bible_alignment: Field names follow Singular naming convention (broker, not brokers)
compliance.api_naming: snake_case for API fields (value, label), camelCase for frontend (value, label)
